independence: high (Wikidata structured creator/author/discoverer record; no model judgment)
scope: Targets the citation_swap STYLE only (a REAL work credited to the wrong creator). Overall pack catch is 10.8% because attribution-swap is ~10 of 60 cases; within that style it catches ~2/3 (the cleanly Wikidata-resolvable works: GR->Hilbert, periodic table->Lavoisier, Mona Lisa->Raphael, penicillin->Pasteur). 0% clean over-block across 3 runs (tight CI). Honest misses: entity-ambiguous works (Hamlet's top search hit is the wrong entity) fail OPEN (unknown, never a fabricated swap), and a genuinely co-credited person (Franklin for DNA) is correctly NOT flagged. Verifies the answer-side attribution against the authoritative record, with HIGH independence — complementary to the citation-existence verifier (fabricated studies) and Google (viral myths).
protocol_note: Run with answer-spec != judge-spec and >=3 runs with a bootstrap 95% CI — the default rigorous protocol the capstone recommends.
